page: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 architecture server instance belonging to the n4d server family. It features 64 dedicated virtual CPUs (vCPUs) paired with 128.0 GB of system memory. This configuration yields a resource density of 2.0 GB of RAM per virtual core, aligning with compute-optimized hardware profiles.

This server type does not include local NVMe SSD storage or integrated graphics processing units (GPUs). All storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Additionally, the instance does not bundle complimentary public IPv4 addresses, meaning external networking must be configured separately.

Given the dedicated allocation of 64 vCPUs and the 1:2 RAM-to-vCPU ratio, the n4d-highcpu-64 is designed for compute-bound workloads. It is structurally suited for tasks such as high-performance web serving, batch processing, distributed analytics, and compilation pipelines where raw CPU capacity is prioritized over large memory footprints. The lack of local storage and GPUs represents a design tradeoff that optimizes cost efficiency for pure CPU-intensive execution.
description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 128.0 GB of system memory. With a resource ratio of 2.0 GB of RAM per core, this instance is designed for CPU-intensive workloads that do not require massive memory footprints. The server does not include local storage or integrated GPUs, meaning all storage needs must be handled via network-attached volumes. This hardware profile is optimized for cost-efficient compute scaling, making it suitable for batch processing, compilation tasks, and high-throughput web serving. The dedicated CPU allocation ensures consistent performance without resource contention, though workloads requiring high-speed local disk I/O or hardware acceleration will need alternative configurations.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform n4d-highcpu-64 is an x86_64 compute-optimized server featuring 64 dedicated vCPUs and 128 GB of RAM, designed for CPU-intensive workloads without local storage or GPU accelerators.

tagline: A dedicated sixty-four core compute-optimized virtual server designed for high-throughput processing workloads requiring minimal memory overhead.
bullet_points: 128.0 GB of system memory yielding 2.0 GB per core
No integrated local storage or GPU hardware
Designed for compute-bound workloads and batch processing
Requires network-attached storage for data persistence
